Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 043 [Royal inscription of Shalmaneser III][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢KUR⸣.KUR-MEŠ ⸢re⸣-ʾu-u ša kal₃ ma-⸢li-ki⸣

[... of] the lands, shepherd of all princes;

o 22

[...]-⸢li?⸣ ša-qu-u-te be-let—iriNINAki

[...] ... elevated, the Lady of Nineveh;

o 33

[...] x x x x-i mi-ša₂-ri

[...] ... justice;

o 44

[...] x kiš-ša₂-⸢te⸣

[...] ... the world;

o 55

[...] x ⸢Aʾ RU?⸣ x x x [...] ⸢GA IŠ⸣1

[...] ... [...] ... .

o 66

[ip]-taq-du-ka dbe-⸢lu₄⸣ da-ni x [... ru]-⸢u₂⸣-qu-⸢te⸣

The gods Bel (and) Anu have entrusted to you the distant ... [...].

o 77

⸢ARAD⸣ ek-ṣu DUMU ma-di-ni ib-ri-šu₂ x [...]

The brazen slave, inhabitant of (Bit)-Adini, (and) his friends, ... [...].

o 88

⸢iri⸣DU₆—bar-si-bi ma-ḫa-zu dan-nu ina dGIRA₂ x x x [...]

Til-Barsip, the strong town, ... [...] in a fire.

o 99

LUGAL-MEŠ ša kurḫat-ti u₂-šaḫ-ri-ru šu-bat-šu₂-nu

They laid waste to the dwellings of the kings of the land Hatti.

o 1010

maš+šur—EN—GUB lu₂tur-ta-nu ka-a-ši li-te iq-bi

Aššur-belu-kaʾʾin, the second-in-command - he (Shalmaneser) spoke to you of victory:

o 1111

iriḪAL.ṢU-MEŠ lu pa-aq-dan-nik-ka lu dan-nat EN.NUN-ka

'May the fortresses be entrusted to you! May your watch be strong!

o 1212

⸢lu⸣-u KALAan ri-kis-ka mu-ḫur bi-lat-su-nu

May your organisation be firm! Receive their tribute!

o 1313

mal-ku ša₂ kurḫat-ti ina ⸢GIR₃⸣-MIN-ia₅ u₂-šak-ni-su-<nu>

I made the princes of Hatti bow down at my feet;

o 1414

lul-lik ša₂ kurur-ar-ṭa-a-a lu-u-mur qa-rab-šu₂-nu

(now) I wish to go (and) experience warfare with the Urarṭians.

o 1515

ša qa₂-aq!(ZI-)qar me-e la A-⸢MEŠ lu⸣-rid ⸢LUḪ⸣ x x NI MU RA

I wish to go down ... [...] ... that it is a region with or without water.

o 1616

i-ṣi-mi₃-di maš+šur—PAB—AŠ kurna-ʾi-ru ⸢MURU₂?⸣ [(...) id?]-de-ke

Aššurnaṣirpal put it under his yoke, (but now) Naʾiru has raised battle.'

o 1717

i-ta-na-am-mar a-na lu₂GAL-MEŠ-šu₂ šip-ṭu ana ⸢lu₂⸣[...] SUMnu

He (Shalmaneser) regularly meets his officers, that they give strict orders to the [...], (saying):

o 1818

šam-⸢ru⸣-te qu-ra-du-u₂-a ina kiṣ?-ri [...] AN

'My furious warriors! Into contingents! [...] ... .

o 1919

ge-er-ri d<<A>>-aš+šur ana šal-la-li al-lak [...] x-ku-nu

I am going on a campaign of Aššur to plunder [...] your [...].

o 2020

lu še-la-a GIR₂-MEŠ AN.BAR šu-uk-nu-[...] x

May (your) iron swords be sharp ... [...] ...!

o 2121

gišGAG.U₄.TAG.GAe še-lu-u-ti ina lib₃-bi x [...] x

[...] sharpened arrows in ... [...]!

o 2222

si-ri AN.BAR ina UGU ANŠE.KUR.RA LUL x [...]

... [...] an iron coat of armour on the horses!

o 2323

gap-ša₂-a-te e-mu-qi-ku-nu la ta-⸢ṭu?⸣-[...]

Do not ... [...] your proud forces!

o 2424

⸢ša⸣ ur-di kurur-ar₂-ṭa-a-a MURU₂ ⸢la⸣ [...]

Do not [...] the battle of the Urarṭian slaves!

o 2525

daš+šur u₂-tak-kil-an-ni u₂-PA-[...]

The god Aššur has encouraged me (and) ... [...].

o 2626

is-sa-aʾ-u UN-MEŠ kuraš+šurki zik-[...]

The people of Assyria have shouted ... [...]:

o 2727

⸢de?-iq?⸣-tu₂ DU₁₀.GA <<LA>> a-dan-niš a-na be-⸢lu₄⸣ [...]

"[...] the very best of luck to the lord.

o 2828

⸢a-lik⸣ be-lu₄ ⸢LUGAL-MEŠ⸣ni ⸢da-aš₂⸣-x [...]

Go, O lord of kings, ... [...]!

o 2929

⸢d⸣MAŠ.MAŠ ina IGI-ka lil-lik d⸢GIRA₂⸣ [...]

May the god Nergal go in front of you! [...] the god Giru [...]!

o 3030

[...] x ina qi₂-bit pi-i-ka ni-im-la-[...]

[...] ... at your command ... [...]!"

o 3131

[...] x-ṣu-u-nu i-ṣa-an-du ša ḫa-an-x [...] x KI

[...] ..., they harnessed ... [...] ... .

o 3232

[...] ⸢ta⸣-ḫa-zi-šu₂ ki-ma na-aḫ-x x x KAL

[...] of his battle like ... [...] ... .

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] x x KUR AN.BAR ⸢mul-mul⸣-lu₄ [...]

[...] iron [...] (and) [...] arrows.

r 22

[...] dan-nu-ti ḫur-ša₂-ni x [...] x [...]

[...] mighty [...] (and) ... [...] mountains [...].

r 33

[...] x GUR GUR ⸢SU⸣ U NU NA uk-ta-a-[...]

[...] ... [...].

r 44

[...] x a-di kursu-ub-ri GIM x [...]

[...] as far as the land of Šubria like ... [...].

r 55

[...] ⸢bi?⸣-ru-ti ⸢it⸣-ti-kil!?(PU₂) ina UGU [...]

He trusted [...] distant [...] on top of [...].

r 66

[...]-e kurur-⸢ar₂-ṭa-a-a ḫa⸣-an-ṭiš x [...]

[...] of the Urarṭians quickly [...].

r 77

[...] ⸢dan⸣-nu-te ak-ta-⸢šad⸣ dan-na-at us-[saḫ-ri]-⸢ib⸣

I have conquered strong [...] (and) devastated fortresses.

r 88

[...]-⸢zu⸣-uz-zu mdaš+šur—PAB—AŠ us-⸢sa?⸣-x x x-šu₂-nu

[...] ... Aššurnaṣirpal ... [...]

r 99

[ina U₄] 1-KAM₂ ša₂ iti⸢KIN⸣ ina IRI LUGALti-[šu₂] ⸢a?⸣-sa-ni-qi₂

[On the] first [day] of Elulu (month VI), I approached [his] royal city.

r 1010

LUGAL-⸢ME ti-iš-re-e it⸣-ta-[...]-bi-šu₂-nu

The kings ... [...] ... .

r 1111

me₂-lam-me ša₂ EN kib-rat i-x-x ina? UGU?-šu₂-nu

The fearsome radiance of the lord of the (four) edges ... [...] on them.

r 1212

e-ta-az-bu IRI-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ina [KUR]e u giš⸢TIR⸣-MEŠ e-ru-bu

They left their cities (and) entered into [mountains] and forests.

r 1313

⸢LU₂⸣ a-⸢a?⸣-um-ma ana na-piš-⸢ti⸣ ana KURe na-an-du-ru-⸢ti?⸣ x x

Every man ... to the very wild mountains to (save his) life.

r 1414

aṣ-bat EGIR-šu₂-nu KURe nam-ra-ṣi aṭ-ru-su-nu

I went after them; I drove them away (into) difficult mountain terrain.

r 1515

18 lim lu₂KUR₂ ina gišTUKUL ta-ḫa-zi uš-ta-mit

I killed 18 thousand enemies with the weapon of battle.

r 1616

ki-i ri-ta-te ina EDIN at-ta-di na-aṣ₂-ma-su-nu

I let their yoked teams loose in the open country instead of (on) the pasture.

r 1717

anšepa-re-e ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠ aḫ-ta-bat ina la ma-ne₂-e ⸢anšea-ga⸣-la

I plundered countless mules, horses, (and) donkeys.

r 1818

NIG₂.GA E₂.GAL-šu₂ U₄ 9-KAM₂ am-ta-ši-iʾ

For 9 days I pillaged the possessions in his palace.

r 1919

ma-du-te da-ad-me-šu₂ ina ŠA₃ IZI as-sa-rap

I burned with fire his many settlements.

r 2020

GEME₂ KUR-šu₂ ina IGIat um-ma-ni-ia!(AT) ar₂-di

I drove the female slaves of his land before my army.

r 2121

⸢e?⸣-ṣi ni-iz-mat IRI LUGALu-ti-šu₂ ina ŠA₃ IZI as-sa-rap

I burned the trees, the desire of his royal city, with fire.

r 2222

ALAM-MEŠni kiš-šu₂-ti-ia ina KURe u ta-ma-te u-ki-in

I erected statues of my authority by mountains and seas.

r 2323

a-di IŠ KA BAR kurna-ʾi-ri ana ⸢kurgil₂-za-a⸣-ni at-ta-rad

I went down as far as the ... of the land of Naʾiru to the land of Gilzanu.

r 2424

ANŠE.KUR.RA ma-da-at-ti am-ḫur iriti-ik-ki iriḫu-bu-uš-ki-a

I received horses as tribute (from) the cities of Tikku (and) Hubuškia.

r 2525

⸢re-eḫ⸣-te ma-da-na-ti at-ta-ḫar iriṭu-ru-⸢uš⸣-pe-e

The rest of the tribute I received (from) the city of Ṭurušpa.

r 2626

x x x [...] x x x-⸢MEŠ⸣-a-ni ana ⸢aš+šur⸣ DINGIR-ia ⸢it-ta⸣-x

... [...] ... to Aššur, my god.

r 2727

ina ri-kis lib₃-⸢bi ana⸣ E₂ x [...] x e-ta-⸢rab⸣

I entered the temple of ... [...] ... with resolve.

r 2828

i-sin-nu ša₂ be-let—iriarba-il₃ x x [...] x KI x ŠID x [...]

... [...] ... the festival of the Lady of Arbaʾil.

r 2929

⸢LUGAL⸣ it-ti ri-⸢ša₂⸣-a-te ina iriBAL.TIL ME? BAD UR.MAḪ [...]

In the city of Baltil, the king ... [...] with delight ... lion.

r 3030

[...] x u₂-šam-qit ⸢it⸣-ti nap-ḫar ⸢bi⸣-lat-šu₂ ⸢ana IGI⸣ d⸢INANA⸣ [...]-⸢rab⸣

He made [...] ... fall, (and) [...] ... before Ištar with all his tribute.

r 3131

[...] x-su-qa IGI-MIN-šu₂ ana ⸢ul⸣-[...] ⸢ik-ta-rab⸣

He blessed [...] ... his eyes to ... [...].

r 3232

[...] x-a ana RA IGI LU ŠE [...] iq-ṭi-aš₂

He gave [...] ... to ... [...].

r 3333

[...] x KU TI MA x [...] x KUR x ⸢a?⸣-mat-⸢ka⸣

[...] ... [...] ... your word.

1$GA# $IŠ# could be ga-mil, (part of) a form of gamālu "do a favour", as interpreted by, e.g., Lambert: ga-mil "sparing", Livingstone: tagammil "[you s]pare"; or Schramm: [... DUG₃].GA-iš "well, kindly".

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2009 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338360
